results.description: ADVANCING ETHIOPIA'S CAPACITY FOR LABORATORY, WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT, SURVEILLANCE, AND EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT TO MEET INTERNATIONAL HEALTH REGULATIONS REQUIREMENTS - CLOSE TO 75% OF THE WORLD?S POPULATION LIVES IN COUNTRIES THAT SCORED BELOW 50 IN THE GLOBAL HEALTH SECURITY INDEX (GHSI). ALL SUB-SAHARAN AFRICAN COUNTRIES, EXCEPT FOR SOUTH AFRICA, SCORED <50 GHSI, BELOW THE WORLD AVERAGE. IN THE PAST 10 YEARS, PARTICULARLY SINCE 2016, THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY (OSU) GLOBAL ONE HEALTH INITIATIVE (GOHI) HAS BEEN CONDUCTING CAPACITY BUILDING ACTIVITIES TO ENHANCE AND STRENGTHEN THE ETHIOPIAN HEALTH SYSTEM.  DURING THIS TIME, GOHI HAS BEEN WORKING WITH IN-COUNTRY PARTNERS AND THE CENTERS FOR DISEASE CONTROL AND PREVENTION (CDC) ON THE GLOBAL HEALTH SECURITY AGENDA (GHSA). SOME OF THE ACHIEVED MILESTONES INCLUDE TRAINING >1,750 PROFESSIONALS; CONDUCTING >27,000 SEROSURVEILLANCE COLLECTIONS/ANALYSES; VACCINATING >56,000 ANIMALS AGAINST RABIES; AND, DEVELOPING A MANUAL TO TRAIN PROFESSIONALS TO COLLECT HIGH QUALITY CLINICAL SPECIMENS. IN ADDITION, AND PRESENTLY, GOHI HAS BEEN AT THE FOREFRONT OF ASSISTING THE GOVERNMENT OF ETHIOPIA IN ITS EFFORT TO RESPOND AGAINST THE CURRENT COVID-19 PANDEMIC.OSU GOHI HAS BEEN A MEMBER OF THE MULTI-SECTORAL TASK FORCE THAT WAS ESTABLISHED BY THE ETHIOPIAN MINISTRY OF HEALTH AND THE ETHIOPIAN PUBLIC HEALTH INSTITUTE TO DEVELOP THE NATIONAL ACTION PLAN FOR HEALTH SECURITY.  GOHI IS A MEMBER OF THE NATIONAL ONE HEALTH STEERING COMMITTEE AND HAS BEEN ACTIVELY ENGAGED IN THE VARIOUS KEY TECHNICAL GROUPS. COMBINED WITH OTHER SYNERGISTIC RESEARCH, TRAINING AND OUTREACH ACTIVITIES FUNDED THROUGH NIH AND THE GATES FOUNDATION, GOHI HAS BEEN WORKING TO BUILD CAPACITIES OF PROFESSIONALS AND INSTITUTIONAL SYSTEMS WITH ACADEMIC, RESEARCH AND REGULATORY INSTITUTIONS, INCLUDING 11 SANDWICH PHD FELLOWS TRAINED AND EIGHT MORE IN THE PIPELINE.FOR THIS CDC NOTICE OF FUNDING OPPORTUNITY (NOFO), THE GOHI ETHIOPIA OFFICE HAS ASSEMBLED A HIGHLY-QUALIFIED, MULTIDISCIPLINARY TECHNICAL AND ADMINISTRATIVE TEAM OF PROFESSIONALS. THE GOHI ETHIOPIA TEAM WILL BE SUPPORTED BY OSU?S MULTIDISCIPLINARY TE AM OF EXPERTS FROM 13 COLLEGES AND CENTERS.  TOGETHER, THE GOHI AND GOHI ETHIOPIA TEAMS ENCOMPASS THE RELEVANT QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE FOR SUCCESSFUL AND IMPACTFUL ACHIEVEMENT OF THE FOUR STRATEGIC AREAS OUTLINED IN THIS NOFO. OSU GOHI IS COMMITTED AND CONFIDENT THAT WE WILL ACCOMPLISH THE STRATEGIC OUTCOMES AND ACHIEVE THE ALIGNED MILESTONES NECESSARY TO ENHANCE ETHIOPIA?S CAPACITY FOR SYSTEMIC ADVANCEMENT OF SURVEILLANCE, LABORATORY OPERATIONS, PUBLIC HEALTH EMERGENCY (PHE) MANAGEMENT, AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT TO HELP THE COUNTRY FULFILL INTERNATIONAL HEALTH REGULATION (IHR) REQUIREMENTS AND INCREASE ITS GLOBAL HEALTH SECURITY INDEX SCORE BY IMPROVING PREVENTION, DETECTION AND RESPONSE SYSTEMS AGAINST PHE.

ALL SUB-SAHARAN AFRICAN COUNTRIES, EXCEPT FOR SOUTH AFRICA, SCORED <50 GHSI, BELOW THE WORLD AVERAGE.
